questions.question: Explain the Cartesian coordinate system.
questions.answer: The Cartesian coordinate system uses two perpendicular axes to locate points using ordered pairs.

questions.question: Describe the position of a point in the first quadrant.
questions.answer: In the first quadrant, both x and y coordinates are positive.

questions.question: Explain how to plot a point on the Cartesian plane.
questions.answer: Move horizontally for x-coordinate and vertically for y-coordinate to mark the point.

questions.question: What are the coordinates of a point lying on the x-axis?
questions.answer: The y-coordinate is zero while x can be any real number.

questions.question: What are the coordinates of a point lying on the y-axis?
questions.answer: The x-coordinate is zero while y can be any real number.

questions.question: Differentiate between x-axis and y-axis.
questions.answer: The x-axis is horizontal and the y-axis is vertical.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the origin.
questions.answer: The origin is the reference point from which all coordinates are measured.

questions.question: In which quadrant does the point (−4, −6) lie?
questions.answer: The point lies in the third quadrant.

questions.question: In which quadrant does the point (5, −2) lie?
questions.answer: The point lies in the fourth quadrant.

questions.question: Why is an ordered pair important?
questions.answer: It gives exact position of a point on the plane.

questions.question: State the sign of x-coordinate to the right of y-axis.
questions.answer: Positive

questions.question: State the sign of y-coordinate above x-axis.
questions.answer: Positive

questions.question: What happens to the coordinates when a point moves left?
questions.answer: The x-coordinate decreases.

questions.question: What happens to the coordinates when a point moves upward?
questions.answer: The y-coordinate increases.

questions.question: Can two different points have the same coordinates?
questions.answer: No, each point has a unique ordered pair.

questions.question: Why is the Cartesian plane divided into four quadrants?
questions.answer: To identify the position of points based on signs of coordinates.

questions.question: Is the point (0, 5) in any quadrant?
questions.answer: No, it lies on the y-axis.

questions.question: Is the point (−3, 0) in any quadrant?
questions.answer: No, it lies on the x-axis.

questions.question: Write one real-life example of coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Locating places on maps.

questions.question: Explain why coordinate geometry is useful.
questions.answer: It helps represent geometric problems algebraically.

questions.question: What is the distance of origin from itself?
questions.answer: Zero

questions.question: Which quadrant contains points with x negative and y positive?
questions.answer: Second quadrant

questions.question: Which quadrant contains points with x positive and y negative?
questions.answer: Fourth quadrant

questions.question: Define horizontal distance in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Distance measured parallel to x-axis.

questions.question: Define vertical distance in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Distance measured parallel to y-axis.
